title: QUIC RFC 9001 TLS KeyUpdate Classifier Verification
scope: Prove that a real post-handshake TLS KeyUpdate message is classified as prohibited and reaches the existing KeyUpdateError close path, while NewSessionTicket remains separately supported and the managed key-phase install path stays distinct.
preconditions: The RFC 9001 requirement-home tests can be discovered and executed locally.
verification_method: Requirement-home tests plus focused execution and trace inspection.
procedure: Run the RFC 9001 Section 6 requirement-home tests for the prohibited TLS KeyUpdate and connection-error clauses.
Confirm the real TLS message-processing seam emits the prohibited-update state for a TLS KeyUpdate message and still emits post-handshake ticket publication for NewSessionTicket.
Render the RFC 9001 SpecTrace markdown siblings and validate the JSON artifacts so the downstream trace reflects the new 0002 leaf slice.
expected_result: The requirement-home tests prove a real post-handshake TLS KeyUpdate is classified and closed through the existing KeyUpdateError path, NewSessionTicket remains separately supported, and the managed key-phase install path is still distinct.
status_summary: This verification slice proves the classifier seam plus the existing KeyUpdateError close path while keeping ticket publication and key-phase installation distinct.

supplemental_sections.heading: Deferred Coverage
supplemental_sections.content: Broad TLS post-handshake behavior, beyond the KeyUpdate classifier and NewSessionTicket support already exercised here, remains out of scope.
